name: mission-control
description: The OPERATE phase owner — what runs a mission AFTER launch. MISSION-CONTROL keeps the live product alive and improving: it verifies operational readiness at go-live, instruments and watches the four golden signals (observability), runs incident response (triage, severity, runbooks, postmortems), keeps a maintenance cadence (dependency upkeep + tech-debt), and closes the loop by turning production signals into a new opportunity that re-enters DISCOVER (the cyclic re-entry). /operate-gate is the front door — an operational-readiness check at go-live and the steady-state health view. Runs standalone on any live project, or advances the idea-to-production lifecycle from OPERATE → DISCOVER when i2p is installed. Carries the KAIZEN self-improvement covenant: every incident becomes a runbook, every alert gap becomes an SLO.

metadata.note: skills/ and commands/ are auto-discovered from the plugin root. /operate-gate is the OPERATE front door; it advances the i2p lifecycle OPERATE → DISCOVER (↻) by capability when i2p is installed.
